The sentence 'If the prop_0, the prop_1' can be interpreted in LTL as 'G (prop_0 -> prop_1)', meaning at all times, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 should also hold. 'In addition, prop_2' is usually interpreted as a requirement that 'prop_2' should always be true, i.e., 'G prop_2'. The conjunction of these two requirements forms the complete specification.

The 'In addition, any prop_2.' can be interpreted as requiring that prop_2 can occur at any time (so, no restriction), but often, in logic, 'any' in this context just asserts 'prop_2' is allowed, i.e., nothing to do.
